comment: Vision inspection rubric (spec 15.2). criteria strings may reference {description}, {silhouette_language}, {vision_style_brief}, {palette}, {forbidden}, {bbox_range} - filled by the prompt builder. applies_to uses category groups expanded by the loader: @mesh = all mesh-producing categories. min_views_for_fail implements the two-view rule (spec 15.3); a defect seen in fewer distinct views must be reported as 'uncertain'.

R1.title: Render sanity
R1.criteria: No view is empty, a single solid color, or unrecognizable garbage. Report pass if every view shows a coherent rendered subject.

R2.title: Texture presence
R2.criteria: No surfaces that are pure black, solid magenta, or showing an obvious placeholder/checker pattern where the design calls for a real material. The deliberate uvcheck_045 view is exempt and labeled. In lit_dark_090 the unlit side is expected to be dark - judge texture presence by the rim-lit region only. Report pass if all surfaces show intentional material.

R3.title: Normal integrity
R3.criteria: In the normals debug views (surface normal rendered as RGB, backfacing surfaces PURE RED): no red regions, and normal colors vary smoothly with surface direction with no large patches inconsistent with neighboring geometry. Report pass if no red and no incoherent normal patches.

R4.title: Texture seams
R4.criteria: No visible UV-seam discontinuities in albedo or shading: no hard color or lighting line where the surface is geometrically continuous. Report pass if surface patterns flow continuously across the geometry.

R5.title: Silhouette and type match
R5.criteria: The object reads unambiguously as: "{description}", in the style: {silhouette_language}. A person seeing only the silhouette should name the object type correctly. Report pass if the type is recognizable and the style language holds.

R6.title: Scale plausibility
R6.criteria: Against the labeled 1 m grey reference cube standing 1.5 m to the asset's left, the object's apparent size is plausible for its description. Expected size range: {bbox_range}. Report pass if apparent size falls in or near that range.

R7.title: Material response
R7.criteria: Materials respond physically plausibly across the lighting rigs: metals show directional specular and pick up the warm light's color; rough surfaces show broad soft highlights; nothing reads as uniform plastic where the design calls for metal, or mirror-like where it should be rough; emissive elements remain visible in the dark rim-lit view. Report pass if material families read correctly under all three rigs.

R8.title: Shading artifacts
R8.criteria: No banding on smooth gradients, no blocky/faceted shading on surfaces meant to be smooth, no striped z-fighting patterns where faces are coplanar, no stretched or smeared texels. Report ZFIGHT_COPLANAR as the defect for striped coplanar flicker (that one is severe); otherwise SHADING_ARTIFACT or BANDING.

R9.title: Structural coherence
R9.criteria: No parts floating disconnected from the body, no parts interpenetrating implausibly, no obviously missing functional pieces (e.g. a handle attached to nothing). Intentional detail elements slightly sunk into the surface are acceptable. Report pass if the assembly is structurally coherent.

R10.title: Tiling seams
R10.criteria: The 3x3 tiled surface shows no visible grid: no seam lines at tile borders and no single obvious landmark feature repeating in a grid pattern. Report pass if the tiling reads as a continuous surface.

R11.title: Skybox integrity
R11.criteria: No pinching or swirl artifacts at the zenith (up_pole) or nadir (down_pole); the horizon is level and continuous across adjacent axis views; no hard vertical seam anywhere. Report pass if the sky reads as continuous in all directions.

R12.title: Theme palette conformance
R12.criteria: Dominant surface colors fall within the theme palette: {palette}. Moderate lighting-induced hue shift is expected and acceptable. None of the forbidden colors ({forbidden}) appear as major surface areas. Overall look matches the style brief: {vision_style_brief}. Report pass unless a dominant color clearly departs from the palette.
